title: viguiera dentata var. helianthoides
intro: Viguiera dentata var. helianthoides, also known as the yellowflower broomweed or false helianthus, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and saponins. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically in the form of a salve or ointment, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies.
